title: How one family and their shipping company dominate Marseille
subhead: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (3/4). Based in France's second-largest city, the company led by Rodolphe Saadé plays a major role in the city but has become less visible since the controversy over excess profits.

title: Starmer rules out leaving Downing Street despite Labour rebellion
Xi set to welcome Trump from a position of strength
Assemblée Nationale inquiry commission exposes deep flaws in France's museum security model
subhead: A report released on May 13 calls for increased resources to secure museums as well as a complete overhaul of the museum leadership appointment process.

title: 'A form of republican downfall': The prosecution's scathing indictment at Sarkozy's appeals trial
subhead: In the initial trial in the Libyan funding case, prosecutors requested that the former French president serve a seven-year prison term. He now faces up to 10 years.

title: The shattered lives of Iranians Manije, Mohammad-Reza and Soroush, all killed by bombings
subhead: Before the Israeli-US bombings began, they were living as retirees and artists in different parts of Tehran. Until they met their deaths in their own homes. Tormented by distress, their loved ones abroad and in Iran recount their last conversations before fate took a tragic turn.

title: Who has the best chance of winning the Palme d'Or?
subhead: This year, 22 films are vying for the top prize at the Cannes Film Festival. Who stands the best chance of winning, judging by the makeup of the selection and the profiles of previous winners?
byline: Marine Delrue,  Adel Miliani and  Pierre Breteau

French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
subhead: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

title: Hantavirus: How great is the transmission risk, as each country sets its own quarantine rules?
subhead: All passengers from the MV Hondius have now been repatriated to their countries of origin. This marks a critical phase, as follow-up and quarantine protocols are not standardized across borders.

title: In Spain, the woman priest challenging the Vatican
subhead: Christina Moreira advocates for women's right to the priesthood, and was excommunicated by the Vatican for seeking the role. She continues her vocation as a parish priest in Galicia, disturbing figures high up in the Catholic Church.
